summary: Excellent front‑of‑house/matrix capture of the legendary Hampton Warlocks shows, highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s, soaring Birdsong and one of the best later‑years Dews.
review: These recordings deliver crystal‑clear FOH sound with rich audience ambience, only marred by a tiny edit in the MAMU. Musically the shows are tight and adventurous – Foolish Heart opens the night, the Birdsong moves listeners to tears, Help > Slip > Franklin’s ignites the crowd, and the Dew ranks among the band’s finest late‑era drum‑driven jams.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of five‑star reviews praise both performance and recording; minor criticisms are few, yielding a high combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house mix captures the full room ambience and audience reaction; crisp instrument separation with rich midi textures. A very small cut in the MAMU is noted but does not detract from overall listening experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong first set builds momentum into a blistering second set; the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence and the Dew provide explosive peaks while the band maintains tight, adventurous playing throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for picking up after The Wheel and delivering classic lead work on Dark Star and other jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland highlighted as “on fire” with outstanding midi work, especially during Slipknot and the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum Gimmie Some Lovin!! and The Dew are singled out as standout drum‑driven moments.
